summary:
Lying down enhances venous return by increasing blood flow towards the heart; however, regional differences mean that lower parts may have reduced perfusion temporarily as they fill up first. The answer aligns best with these physiological changes observed during positional shifts. \boxed{D}

The summary captures both critical aspects—enhanced venous return facilitating overall circulation but also noting differential effects based on anatomical positioning within the respiratory system—and directly connects them back to why Option \(D\) would be correct for describing what happens physiologically under such conditions. This encapsulates Agent’s primary rationale succinctly while addressing all pertinent details necessary per your instructions. 

---
